commit.message:
feat: smart daily maintenance system for cost-effective scraping

- Add daily-maintenance.js: 4-phase automated system running at 3AM CET
  Phase 1: Agency re-detection (free)
  Phase 2: URL validation for expired listings (free)
  Phase 3: Incremental scrape - only last 48h listings, 12 locations (from 44)
  Phase 4: Phone number enrichment via direct HTTP (free)
- Extract shared modules: agency-detector.js, apify-client.js
- Refactor scraper.js to use shared modules, remove old auto-scheduler
- Add maintenance routes: GET/POST /api/scraper/maintenance/status|run
- Add validated_at, phone_checked_at fields to property model
- Remove aggressive auto-resume bigrun on startup
- Target: $9-15/month Apify usage (down from $85)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable auto message fetching so incoming messages are received

MeshCore firmware queues incoming messages on the device. Without
calling start_auto_message_fetching(), they were never pulled down,
so CONTACT_MSG_RECV events never fired and RX was silent.

commit.message:
Lepton-lite JPEG DCT preprocessor: -12.3% on earth.jpg

Extracts quantized DCT coefficients from baseline JPEG, reorders by
frequency band (all DCs together, then AC[0], AC[1], ...) so spatially
correlated values become adjacent for better CM context modeling.
Varint coefficient encoding, bit-exact roundtrip verified.

Integrated into both sequential and parallel compression pipelines
(TransformType=3). CLI flag: -nojpeg to disable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent four-call merge clobbering and remap field status names

parseStructuredResponse() returns ALL fields with empty defaults, so
Object.assign merges from later calls overwrote earlier calls' real data
(e.g. light call's empty HQ overwrote locations call's real HQ). Fix by
introducing field-ownership filtering via pickKeys() — each call only
merges/saves its owned fields. Also remap field_statuses short names
(headquarters, manufacturing, keywords) to long names expected by
runDirectEnrichment().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

for saving a LabelsSet. Supports both array and record input formats.

commit.message:
WP2: Wire orphaned DMN tables and add phase boundary patterns (#25)

* WP2: Wire orphaned DMN tables, add phase boundary patterns

- Wire DMN-4 (AutomationTierAssignment) into Phase 2 as Task 2.3a
- Wire DMN-5 (AgentConfidenceEscalation) into Phase 3 after agent assessments
- Fix DMN-4 input vocabulary to align with DMN-1 output labels
- Add phase boundary pattern to Phase 1 (quality gate + signal end event)
- Add phase boundary pattern to Phase 2 (merge gateway + quality gate + signal end event)
- Phase 4 and Phase 7 already compliant (signal end events present)
- Phase 8 is terminal (no phase transition needed)
- Extend Phase 2 pool/lane widths to accommodate boundary pattern elements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #25 review findings: timer label, BRT candidateGroups, boundary flowNodeRef

- Phase 1: Change QG timer label from ISO format (P2D) to human-readable (2 Day SLA)
- Phase 1: Widen timer BPMNLabel to 46px to fit "2 Day SLA" text
- Phase 2: Remove candidateGroups from businessRuleTask (no runtime effect in Camunda 7)
- Phase 2: Remove boundary event from lane flowNodeRef (consistent with Phase 1/3/5 pattern)
- Finding 4 (missing completion gateway) is a false positive — matches Phase 5 reference model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix vendor sequencing on correct v4 base and codify modeling patterns

Task 1: Re-apply vendor sequencing fix using text-based edits (not lxml)
on the user's authoritative v4 file. Inserts Receive_VendorResponse and
Receive_SignedContract receive tasks with P5D/P7D boundary timers,
swaps DD task order, and shifts downstream DI elements. Element count:
161 → 171 (+10 as expected). Activity_0zf4l0g and Flow_0033t8v preserved.

Task 2: Codify v3→v4 modeling patterns into rules:
- Gateway naming convention (question-style with "?")
- Conditional flow labels (always Yes/No, never action-specific)
- Cross-lane notification pattern (notify initiator before end)
- Regulatory annotation approach (camunda:properties over textAnnotation)
- Lane ordering convention (process-facing at top)
- Minimum lane heights per content density
- BPMN file editing safety lessons (never use lxml round-trip)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove invalid candidateGroups from receiveTask elements

camunda:candidateGroups is a userTask-only attribute; receiveTask
elements wait for messages and don't support candidate group assignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix SLA breach end events: reposition within pool boundaries

Move End_VendorResponseSLABreach and End_ContractSLABreach from below
the boundary timers (y=1092, overflowing pool at y=1121) to beside them
(y=1052, within pool). Timer escalation flows changed from 4px vertical
to 44px horizontal for visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive Quick Start Guide

New file: QUICKSTART.md - Get started in 5 minutes

Sections:
1. Installation (2 minutes) - Clone, install dependencies, verify
2. Train Best Model (3 minutes) - MSTCN training with optimal settings
3. View Results - Logs, visualizations, W&B dashboard
4. Compare Models (optional) - Benchmark top 3 models
5. Production Deployment - Basic inference example

Features:
- Step-by-step commands with expected outputs
- Troubleshooting section (common issues + solutions)
- Next steps (explore models, try datasets, tune hyperparameters)
- Performance expectations table (all 20 models)
- Key takeaways (dos and don'ts)

Optimized for new users to get results quickly with best practices built-in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(workload): map inference-perf enable_multi_turn_chat to single-session mode (#517)

* feat(workload): map inference-perf enable_multi_turn_chat to single-session mode (#514)

Three changes to the workload generator:

1. Fix pre-existing bug: reasoning path now respects lifecycle windows
   (parity with standard path). Per-round filtering ensures rounds
   outside lifecycle windows are suppressed (BC-6).

2. Add SingleSession mode to MultiTurnSpec: each client creates exactly
   one session instead of spawning new sessions per arrival. Used by
   inference-perf expansion to model persistent chat sessions.

3. Compute MaxRounds and ThinkTimeUs from stage parameters instead of
   hardcoding (MaxRounds=ceil(R*D/N), ThinkTimeUs=floor(N/R*1e6)).
   ContextGrowth set to "" (fixed-length) per H30 finding — real
   inference-perf sends constant input tokens, not accumulated context.

Enables multi-stage + multi-turn (removes explicit rejection).

Fixes #514

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(examples): update stale enable_multi_turn_chat comments from #513

The upstream #513 described the flag as "accepted but ignored". Since
#514 implements the mapping, update the example YAML comments to
reference the actual behavior (SingleSession mode).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add deeply nested config tests for Commander unit and acceptance tests

Exercises multi-level config trees (server.connection.host, etc.)
through both mock-based unit tests and real Commander acceptance tests.
Acceptance tests verify the full round-trip from defaults through

commit.message:
Fix TypeScript errors in useInventory Supabase calls

Cast insert/update/upsert payloads to bypass strict typed-client
resolution that treats user_inventory table operations as never.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix break overlay not appearing on manual session completion

The overlay was incorrectly gated behind `auto && autostartBreaks`,
so it never showed when the user manually clicked ✓. The autostartBreaks
flag only controls behaviour inside the overlay (auto-timer vs prompt),
not whether the overlay appears at all. Now shows on every completion
unless it's the first part of a split session handing off to the next.

commit.message:
Update CLAUDE.md with benchmark findings and best practices

Major updates:
- Update best model: MSTCN (RMSE 6.80, R² 0.90) replaces CNN-GRU
- Add comprehensive "Best Practices" section from 20-model benchmark
  - Model selection guidance (production vs speed)
  - Critical hyperparameters (sequence length most important!)
  - Training recommendations with examples
  - Key learnings (shorter sequences win, attention matters, RNNs obsolete)
- Update Related Documentation with new analysis reports
- Update Common Pitfalls with sequence length warning

Key insights documented:
- Sequence length 1K >> 20K (58% improvement!)
- MSTCN/Transformer/WaveNet are top tier
- Traditional RNNs have negative R² on this dataset
- 30 epochs sufficient (100 epochs only 6% better)

fix: add --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern to lint script

ESLint v9 exits with code 2 when a glob pattern (*.cjs) matches no files.
In CI the untracked root .cjs files don't exist, causing lint to fail.
Flag suppresses the error while still linting any .cjs files present locally.
